titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Oscar Wilde Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.abbr: beinecke manuscript unit archival processing manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday August 7, 2018 at 21:03
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.
beinecke.wilde.xml converted for compliance with Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines with brbl-migrate-01.xsl (mr2007-08-13).
PUBLIC "-//Yale University::Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library//TEXT (US::CtYBR::::[OSCAR WILDE COLLECTION ])//EN" "wilde.xml" converted from EAD 1.0 to 2002 by v1to02.xsl (sy2003-10-15).

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Letters by Oscar Wilde to various parties, including Leonard Smithers and Frank Harris; letters between persons acquainted with Wilde, several manuscripts of writings by Wilde, including a notebook on Roman history, notes on travels in France and Spain, and "Phrases and Philosophies for the use of the young"; and several ephemeral items relating to Wilde.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Ongoing collection of documents acquired by gift and purchase from various sources. Type of accession (gift or purchase) and date of acquisition is noted in the box-and-folder list. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Oscar Wilde Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Oscar Wilde Collection. General Collection. Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This finding aid was produced from previously existing cards in the Manuscripts Catalog, or from another inventory. All pertinent bibliographical information has been retained. This finding aid will be updated periodically to account for new materials that may be added to the collection. The date of the most recent update is noted on the title page. For information on material that may have been added since the last update, please consult the Public Service Desk.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: Microfilm is available for folder 24: Notebook containing notes on Roman history and draft of "Vera, or, the Nihilists." Consult Public Services staff.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This finding aid encompasses accessions of single manuscripts and small groups of manuscripts and other papers, by or pertaining to Oscar Wilde, which the library has chosen to group and list as an artificial collection. Materials incorporated into the collection prior to June 1997 have been organized into three series. The first series consists of items from the bequest of the estate of Katherine Dreier, principally letters removed from books (cataloged separately). The second series contains items given as a group by Abel Cary Thomas, mainly letters written by Wilde towards the end of his life. The third series, Additional Materials, contains two letters and several manuscripts, including notes by Wilde on his travels in France and Spain, and a draft of his "Phrases and Philosophies for the use of the young."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1954

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by photographs of Cyril Holland and Vyvian Holland; Removed from: Sherard, Robert Harborough. The Real Oscar Wilde , London 1916, Ip.w644.w916

did.unittitle: ALS to Thomas Werner Laurie, Norwood

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from his: The Real Oscar Wilde , London, 1902, Ip.W644.W914

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from: Sherard, Robert Harborough. Oscar Wilde , London, 1902, Ip.W644.W902S

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from: Sherard, Robert Harborough. Oscar Wilde , London, 1902, Ip.W644.W902S

did.unittitle: ALS to Charles Godfrey Leland, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by a typescript and a photograph of Wilde in 1878; bound in dark blue full morocco

did.unittitle: ALS to Sir John Pentland Mahaffy, Chelsea

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from: Sherard, Robert Harborough. Oscar Wilde , London, 1902, Ip.W644.W902S

did.unittitle: ALS to Robert Baldwin Ross, Paris

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from: Sherard, Robert Harborough. Oscar Wilde , London, 1902, Ip.W644.W902S

did.unittitle: 6 ALS and 4 telegrams (copies) to Leonard Smithers, London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The letters of 1897, Nov 16 and Dec 6 were removed from his: "The Ballad of Reading Gaol," 1899, Ip. W644.W898Bc.; The remaining letters and telegrams were removed from Sherard, Robert Harborough. Oscar Wilde , London, 1902, Ip.W644.W902S

did.unittitle: Extrait des minutes des actes de décès: a copy of the police department's official report of Oscar Wilde's death, Paris. Préfecture de la Seine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from: Sherard, Robert Harborough. Oscar Wilde , London, 1902 Ip.W644.S902S

did.unittitle: Oscar Wilde's last hotel bill, made put to M. Melmouth, Hotel d'Alsace, Jean Dupoirier, Paris

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from: Sherard, Robert Harborough. Oscar Wilde , London, 1902, Ip.W644.W902S

did.unittitle: Materials given by Abel Cary Thomas
ALS to [Mrs. Bernard Beere] telling of his lodging place, and asking for news, Dieppe

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed with an assumed name, Sebastian Melmouth

did.unittitle: ALS to Bernstein, asking him to bring three copies of the "Ballad" to be signed, Paris

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typewritten copy laid in

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: [02/14/2000: Letter appears to be a forgery]

did.unittitle: 3 ALS to Frank [Harris] the first and second thanking him for money and clothes, the third asking him for money with which to pay the hotel bill, Dieppe, Paris

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by typewritten copies

did.unittitle: ALS to Pierre Louÿs, in French, [London]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by typewritten copy

did.unittitle: 4 ALS to his London publisher, Leonard Smithers, chiefly asking for money, [Paris?]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typewritten copies of 3 letters laid in

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: [02/14/2000: Last letter appears to be a forgery]

did.unittitle: ALS to Henry-D. Davray expressing delight that his poem was to appear with Davray's translation [probably referring to the "Ballad of Reading Goal"], [Paris]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by a typewritten copy

did.unittitle: ALS to "Harold" acknowledging some communication and asking Harold to come that evening, Magdalen College, Oxford

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typewritten copy laid in

did.unittitle: "Latin Verses:" holograph copy of four lines in Latin with the name "Oscar Wilde" at the end

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by a typewritten copy of the verse, begins "Laurus cara mihi dando. . . "

did.unittitle: "To my friend Luther Munday:" holograph copy signed by Oscar Wilde

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published with title"Impression du matin" The World , 1881 Mar 2; reproduced in facsimile in the Picture Magazine , 1895 Feb 26, with the title, "To my friend Luther Munday"; Accompanied by a typewritten copy

did.unittitle: A photograph [Sarony], a reproduction of a photograph [J. Jacobs, London], and a reproduction of a drawing of Oscar Wilde [Mayer, Merkel, Ottman, New York]
Typescript letter (transcript) to an unidentified recipient, Chelsea, S. W.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Removed from: Wilde, Oscar, 1854- 1900. Happy prince and other tales by Oscar Wilde ; illustrated by Walter Crane and Jacomb Hood, London: David Nutt; London and Edinburgh: Ballantyne Press; Ballantyne, Hanson and Co., 1888

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1983

did.unittitle: Bill, in French, from Auld Reekie, Scotch Tailors, Paris

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1962

did.unittitle: Accompanied with typed card, signed by Randolph H. Hill, describing this typescript as one of several copies made and "circulated privately in Springfield, Massacusetts, subsequent to its publication"; Published by Leonard Smithers, London, 1898; and B.R. Tucker, New York, 1899

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes on Roman history: miscellaneous notes, possibly unpublished; "Vera, or, the Nihilists": early draft of the play privately printed in 1880; and 2 p. of aphorisms on love

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1953

did.unittitle: [Notes on travels in France and Spain], holograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ex libris Richard Le Gallienne; laid in col: (1) transcript, typewritten of holograph; (2) 1 negative photostat of p. 2 of holograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1983

did.unittitle: "O loved one lying far away:" holograph ms. poem, 6 stanzas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by TLS from Arthur Brentano and reproduction of sketch of Wilde, 1878

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Source unknown

did.unittitle: Phrases and Philosophies for the use of the young: holograph, signed, Albemarle Club, London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by: "The Chameleon", London: Gay and Bird, vol. 1, no. 1, 1894. No. 57 of an edition of 100 numbered copies. 1 vol. in a case

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1983

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This poem, with revision and an extra stanza, was published under the title "Under the Balcony" in the Shakespearean Show Book, Manchester; accompanied by: typescript of a review published in Pall Mall Gazette, 1884 Jun 3

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gift, 1983
